name: social-skills
description: Social-web skills for AI agents. Bundles HackerNews / Reddit / GitHub / X / LinkedIn / YouTube / Bluesky / arXiv / Medium / Substack / RSS / generic article fetchers, plus search (DuckDuckGo, Brave, SerpAPI, Tavily, Perplexity, X, HN, Reddit, YouTube, Bluesky, arXiv, Google CSE), ask (Perplexity, Grok, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Tavily, SerpAPI), and a local SQLite-backed ledger of every fetched URL.
long_description: social-skills equips AI agents with structured access to the social web. Bundles two binaries: social-fetch (MCP server exposing fetch / search / ask / timeline / research / list_providers / bridge_status / read_file plus seven social_ledger_* tools and six social_ledger_influencers_* tools for tracking topic authorities) and social-ledger (auto-detected SQLite + FTS5 store that caches every fetch so 'have we seen this URL?' answers are instant without re-fetching). Big-payload tools (fetch / ask / research / ledger_get) write the rendered body to a temp file and return a small JSON envelope with metadata + a content_file path — keeps article bodies off the JSON-RPC encode path. Provider chains (-p auto) gracefully fall through providers that lack keys or hit rate limits.

tools.name: social_fetch_fetch
tools.description: Fetch content at a URL (HN, Reddit, GitHub, X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Bluesky, arXiv, Medium, Substack, RSS, generic). Returns a small envelope + content_file (read with social_fetch_read_file). Includes a `media[]` array of {url, type, alt} for any images / diagrams / video posters in the post — the agent's vision tool reads each media URL directly when the user asks about visual content. Adds a `hint` field when extraction looks thin (likely JS-rendered SPA).

tools.name: social_fetch_search
tools.description: Search the web or a specific platform. Supports -p auto fallback chains. PAGINATION: offset-paginated providers (serpapi, serpapi-news, hackernews, arxiv, brave, google) take `start: N`; cursor-paginated providers (reddit, x, youtube, bluesky) take `cursor: <token>` and the response includes `next_cursor` to pass back. The `serpapi-news` variant queries Google's News tab.

tools.name: social_fetch_ask
tools.description: Ask a grounded answer engine (Perplexity, Grok, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Tavily, SerpAPI). Answer body lives in content_file; sources stay inline.

tools.name: social_fetch_timeline
tools.description: Recent activity for a user on X or LinkedIn.

tools.name: social_fetch_list_providers
tools.description: Discover available providers at runtime PLUS their availability — each entry has {name, status, missing}, where status is 'ok' (ready), 'missing-auth' (env vars not set; `missing` lists which) or 'needs-bridge' (requires the local browser bridge). Call this first to see which providers to use; do NOT invoke ones with missing-auth.

tools.name: social_fetch_bridge_status
tools.description: Probe the local browser bridge (LinkedIn / Medium / Substack).

tools.name: social_fetch_research
tools.description: EXPERIMENTAL — multi-angle research workflow: decomposes a question, fans out parallel fetch/search/ask/timeline, synthesizes a markdown answer with citations. Report body lives in content_file.

tools.name: social_fetch_hints
tools.description: Read platform-specific quirks / gotchas / rate-limit notes (X 7-day search cap, LinkedIn rate-limit risk, Reddit anonymous-search relevance trade-off, etc.). Call BEFORE running a search/fetch on a platform you haven't used recently. With no `platform` argument returns ALL hints concatenated; with one returns just that platform.

tools.name: social_fetch_read_file
tools.description: Read a content_file produced by an earlier social_fetch_* / social_ledger_* call. Chunked (default 64 KB) so large bodies can be paged. Locked to the system temp dir + social-fetch- prefix.

tools.name: social_ledger_seen
tools.description: Check whether one or more URLs are already cached in the local ledger. Returns {url, seen, source, fetched_at, provenance, canonical_url} per URL — provenance + freshness inline so the agent can decide whether to re-fetch without a follow-up `get`. Use BEFORE fetching.

tools.name: social_ledger_get
tools.description: Retrieve one stored item from the ledger by URL — markdown body + provenance ('auto-fetched' = high trust, social_fetch_* ingested it; 'agent-recorded' = trust depends on what the agent fed in).

tools.name: social_ledger_list
tools.description: List ledger items, newest first. Filter by source / recency window.

tools.name: social_ledger_search
tools.description: FTS5 search the ledger (BM25-ranked) across title / summary / content / author / tags.

tools.name: social_ledger_stats
tools.description: Counts, sizes, oldest/newest in the ledger.

tools.name: social_ledger_record
tools.description: Store one URL+content pair in the ledger (e.g. after a Claude WebFetch). Refused when SOCIAL_LEDGER_READONLY=1.

tools.name: social_ledger_forget
tools.description: Drop one item from the ledger. Refused when SOCIAL_LEDGER_READONLY=1.

anthropic_api_key.title: Anthropic API key
anthropic_api_key.description: Claude ask provider — get one at console.anthropic.com

openai_api_key.title: OpenAI API key
openai_api_key.description: OpenAI ask provider — get one at platform.openai.com

gemini_api_key.title: Gemini API key (`gemini` ask provider)
gemini_api_key.description: For the `gemini` ask provider. Easiest path: aistudio.google.com → Get API key → Create key → copy. Free tier: 1,500 req/day on gemini-2.5-flash. Gemini's built-in google_search tool grounds answers with citations.

google_api_key.title: Google API key (Custom Search)
google_api_key.description: For the `google` search provider (Custom Search API only). Get from console.cloud.google.com — enable the Custom Search API on your project. Note: Google removed 'Search the entire web' for new CSEs in 2024 — google search is now restricted to your listed sites; prefer serpapi/brave/tavily for general web search. (Gemini ask uses GEMINI_API_KEY; YouTube uses YOUTUBE_API_KEY — three independent keys.)

google_cse_id.title: Google Custom Search Engine ID
google_cse_id.description: Required for the `google` search provider. Get from programmablesearchengine.google.com

tavily_api_key.title: Tavily API key
tavily_api_key.description: Tavily ask + search — tavily.com (1k searches/mo free)

serpapi_key.title: SerpAPI key
serpapi_key.description: SerpAPI search + Google AI Overview ask — serpapi.com (100/mo free)

x_api_key.title: X / Twitter API key
x_api_key.description: X API key (paired with secret) — developer.x.com. NOT the bearer token.

youtube_api_key.title: YouTube Data API key
youtube_api_key.description: Optional — YouTube fetch metadata works without it; needed for comments + search.

github_token.title: GitHub token
github_token.description: Optional — raises rate limit from 60/hr to 5,000/hr.

bluesky_handle.title: Bluesky handle
bluesky_handle.description: Required for Bluesky search (e.g. you.bsky.social). Bluesky fetch works without it.

bluesky_app_password.title: Bluesky app password
bluesky_app_password.description: Generate at bsky.app/settings/app-passwords — NOT your account password.

jina_api_key.title: Jina API key (optional)
jina_api_key.description: Optional — Jina Reader free tier works without a key. Set for higher rate limits.

html2md_reader.title: HTML→Markdown reader
html2md_reader.description: local (default) or jina (route generic article fetches via r.jina.ai — handles Cloudflare-blocked / JS-rendered pages)

tavily_topic.title: Tavily topic
tavily_topic.description: Set to 'news' for strict date filtering (narrower recall). Empty/general for default.

social_ledger.title: Ledger mode
social_ledger.description: auto / 1 / 0. Default `auto` enables the ledger when social-ledger is bundled (always true in this .mcpb). `1` = explicit on; `0` = explicit off. The ledger caches every fetch in SQLite + FTS5 so 'have we seen this URL?' is instant.

social_ledger_dir.title: Ledger storage directory
social_ledger_dir.description: Override where social-ledger stores its SQLite + mirror tree. Default: $XDG_DATA_HOME/social-ledger or ~/.local/share/social-ledger.

social_ledger_readonly.title: Ledger read-only mode
social_ledger_readonly.description: `1` disables ledger writes (record / forget / auto-ingest). Reads (seen / get / list / search / stats) keep working. Default `0` = writes allowed. Useful for research-only agent profiles that should consult but not mutate the ledger.

social_fetch_audit.title: Audit log
social_fetch_audit.description: `0` disables the always-on audit log; `1` (default) enables it. Audit lines record every fetch / MCP tool call for `social-fetch monitor`.

social_bridge_url.title: Browser-bridge URL
social_bridge_url.description: Local Chrome-bridge daemon endpoint. Default: http://127.0.0.1:5555/cmd. Change when running multiple sandboxed browser profiles or forwarding to a remote machine.
